channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: When Cow Urine Cures Constipation on YouTube: Limits of LLMs in Detecting Culture-specific Health Misinformation
item.description: arXiv:2604.22002v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Social media platforms have become primary channels for health information in the Global South. Using gomutra (cow urine) discourse on YouTube in India as a case study, we present a post-facto Large Language Model (LLM)-assisted discourse analysis of ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, model, arxiv, analysis, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Shared Lexical Task Representations Explain Behavioral Variability In LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2604.22027v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: One of the most common complaints about large language models (LLMs) is their prompt sensitivity -- that is, the fact that their ability to perform a task or provide a correct answer to a question can depend unpredictably on the way the question is po...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, product, model, example, demonstration | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Source-Modality Monitoring in Vision-Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.22038v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We define and investigate source-modality monitoring -- the ability of multimodal models to track and communicate the input source from which pieces of information originate. We consider source-modality monitoring as an instance of the more general bi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, multimodal, model, arxiv, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Lightweight Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Large Language Model-Based Modeling for Scalable Patient-Trial Matching
item.description: arXiv:2604.22061v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Patient-trial matching requires reasoning over long, heterogeneous electronic health records (EHRs) and complex eligibility criteria, posing significant challenges for scalability, generalization, and computational efficiency. Existing approaches eith...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, model, arxiv, reasoning, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Outcome Rewards Do Not Guarantee Verifiable or Causally Important Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.22074v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) on chain-of-thought reasoning has become a standard part of language model post-training recipes. A common assumption is that the reasoning chains trained through RLVR reliably represent how a mode...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, reasoning, chain-of-thought, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An End-to-End Ukrainian RAG for Local Deployment. Optimized Hybrid Search and Lightweight Generation
item.description: arXiv:2604.22095v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents a highly efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system built specifically for Ukrainian document question answering, which achieved 2nd place in the UNLP 2026 Shared Task. Our solution features a custom two-stage search pip...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, retrieval, RAG, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Knowledge-driven Augmentation and Retrieval for Integrative Temporal Adaptation
item.description: arXiv:2604.22098v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time introduces fundamental challenges in model development and deployment: models are usually trained on historical data while deployed on future data where semantic distributions and domain knowledge may evolve. Unfortunately, existing studies eithe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, retrieval, RAG, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Where Should LoRA Go? Component-Type Placement in Hybrid Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.22127v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hybrid language models that interleave attention with recurrent components are increasingly competitive with pure Transformers, yet standard LoRA practice applies adapters uniformly without considering the distinct functional roles of each component t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, transformer, model, attention, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Read the Paper, Write the Code: Agentic Reproduction of Social-Science Results
item.description: arXiv:2604.21965v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work has used LLM agents to reproduce empirical social science results with access to both the data and code. We broaden this scope by asking: Can they reproduce results given only a paper's methods description and original data? We develop an ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, arxiv, analysis, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sound Agentic Science Requires Adversarial Experiments
item.description: arXiv:2604.22080v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based agents are rapidly being adopted for scientific data analysis, automating tasks once limited by human time and expertise. This capability is often framed as an acceleration of discovery, but it also accelerates a familiar failure mode, the r...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, arxiv, analysis, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Memanto: Typed Semantic Memory with Information-Theoretic Retrieval for Long-Horizon Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.22085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The transition from stateless language model inference to persistent, multi session autonomous agents has revealed memory to be a primary architectural bottleneck in the deployment of production grade agentic systems. Existing methodologies largely de...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, model, arxiv, memory, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Emergent Strategic Reasoning Risks in AI: A Taxonomy-Driven Evaluation Framework
item.description: arXiv:2604.22119v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As reasoning capacity and deployment scope grow in tandem, large language models (LLMs) gain the capacity to engage in behaviors that serve their own objectives, a class of risks we term Emergent Strategic Reasoning Risks (ESRRs). These include, but a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, reasoning, LLM, large language model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Does LLM Self-Correction Help? A Control-Theoretic Markov Diagnostic and Verify-First Intervention
item.description: arXiv:2604.22273v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Iterative self-correction is widely used in agentic LLM systems, but when repeated refinement helps versus hurts remains unclear. We frame self-correction as a cybernetic feedback loop in which the same language model serves as both controller and pla...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, arxiv, prompting, LLM, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Introducing Background Temperature to Characterise Hidden Randomness in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.22411v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Even when decoding with temperature $T=0$, large language models (LLMs) can produce divergent outputs for identical inputs. Recent work by Thinking Machines Lab highlights implementation-level sources of nondeterminism, including batch-size variation,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, large language model, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Focus Session: Hardware and Software Techniques for Accelerating Multimodal Foundation Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.21952v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work presents a multi-layered methodology for efficiently accelerating multimodal foundation models (MFMs). It combines hardware and software co-design of transformer blocks with an optimization pipeline that reduces computational and memory requ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, arxiv, memory, fine-tuning, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Universal Transformers Need Memory: Depth-State Trade-offs in Adaptive Recursive Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.21999v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study learned memory tokens as computational scratchpad for a single-block Universal Transformer (UT) with Adaptive Computation Time (ACT) on Sudoku-Extreme, a combinatorial reasoning benchmark. We find that memory tokens are empirically necessary:...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> scratchpad, arxiv, memory, reasoning, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LayerBoost: Layer-Aware Attention Reduction for Efficient LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2604.22050v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transformers are mostly relying on softmax attention, which introduces quadratic complexity with respect to sequence length and remains a major bottleneck for efficient inference. Prior work on linear or hybrid attention typically replaces softmax att...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, memory, transformer, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, context window, prompt engineering, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.
item.description: A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, model, prompt engineering, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> audio, reasoning, chain-of-thought, framework, CoT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, retrieval, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, retrieval, reasoning, LLM, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: graphrag - A modular graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.
item.description: SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, retrieval, RAG, augmented, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Optimal Question Selection from a Large Question Bank for Clinical Field Recovery in Conversational Psychiatric Intake
item.description: arXiv:2604.22067v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Psychiatric intake is a sequential, high-stakes information-gathering process in which clinicians must decide what to ask, in what order, and how to interpret incomplete or ambiguous responses under limited time. Despite growing interest in conversati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, LLM, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Rethinking Publication: A Certification Framework for AI-Enabled Research
item.description: arXiv:2604.22026v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI research pipelines now produce a growing share of publishable academic output, including work that meets existing peer-review standards for quality and novelty. Yet the publication system was built on the assumption of universal human authorship an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, research, framework, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mochi: Aligning Pre-training and Inference for Efficient Graph Foundation Models via Meta-Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.22031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose Mochi, a Graph Foundation Model that addresses task unification and training efficiency by adopting a meta-learning based training framework. Prior models pre-train with reconstruction-based objectives such as link prediction, and assume th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, framework, few-shot, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Kernel Contracts: A Specification Language for ML Kernel Correctness Across Heterogeneous Silicon
item.description: arXiv:2604.22032v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Every ML kernel ships with an implicit contract about what it computes. People rarely write the contract down. When two kernels disagree -- when a matmul on AMD produces a different gradient than the same matmul on NVIDIA, when a fused attention kerne...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, attention, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: fastapi_mcp - Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!
item.description: Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, context, API, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, reasoning, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: When Quotes Crumble: Detecting Transient Mechanical Liquidity Erosion in Limit Order Books
item.description: arXiv:2604.21993v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study the detection of transient liquidity erosion ("crumbling quotes") in electronic limit order books, where observable quote deterioration may reflect either mechanical liquidity withdrawal or informational repricing. Using the ABIDES agent-base...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, model, experiment, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, example, prompt engineering, step-by-step, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Incentivizing Neuro-symbolic Language-based Reasoning in VLMs via Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.22062v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: There are 7,407 languages in the world. But, what about the languages that are not there in the world? Are humans so narrow minded that we don't care about the languages aliens communicate in? Aliens are humans too! In the 2016 movie Arrival, Amy Adam...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, reasoning, model, vision, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An Artifact-based Agent Framework for Adaptive and Reproducible Medical Image Processing
item.description: arXiv:2604.21936v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medical imaging research is increasingly shifting from controlled benchmark evaluation toward real-world clinical deployment. In such settings, applying analytical methods extends beyond model design to require dataset-aware workflow configuration and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, research, image, library, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Performance Anomaly Detection in Athletics: A Benchmarking System with Visual Analytics
item.description: arXiv:2604.21953v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Anti-doping programs rely on biological testing to detect performance-enhancing drugs, but such testing costs over $800 per sample and is limited by short detection windows for many prohibited substances. These constraints leave large portions of athl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, ICL, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Conditional anomaly detection using soft harmonic functions: An application to clinical alerting
item.description: arXiv:2604.21956v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Timely detection of concerning events is an important problem in clinical practice. In this paper, we consider the problem of conditional anomaly detection that aims to identify data instances with an unusual response, such as the omission of an impor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, example, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LTBs-KAN: Linear-Time B-splines Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
item.description: arXiv:2604.22034v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) are a recent neural network architecture offering an alternative to Multilayer Perceptrons (MLPs) with improved explainability and expressibility. However, KANs are significantly slower than MLPs due to the recursive ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, arxiv, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
